description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a small crustacean, likely a crayfish or similar species, with a segmented body featuring a robust cephalothorax and a flexible abdomen. The exoskeleton is a muted brownish-tan color, with subtle gradations and naturalistic texturing that mimics the rough, slightly matte surface of a real crustacean shell. Prominent features include two large, asymmetrical claws (chelae) at the front, several pairs of articulated walking legs, and a fan-shaped tail at the rear. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the cephalothorax, and long, thin antennae extend forward. The overall form is compact and slightly arched, with the legs splayed outward for stability. The object is small, as indicated by the nearby color calibration cube, and would fit easily in the palm of a hand.
description: A small, brownish-tan crustacean with segmented body, large claws, and splayed legs, featuring realistic texturing and anatomical detail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, both claws, and the curvature of the tail.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the segmentation of the body, arrangement of legs, and symmetry of the claws.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the other profile of the body and claws, with antennae visible.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the claws, eye stalks, and forward-projecting antennae.
